summary.intent: Address two rounds of Codex automated code review findings on PR #704 (RangeCalendar component in fogodev/ars-ui): push fixes, resolve review threads, monitor CI, and respond to a second Codex pass with targeted regression tests.
summary.outcome: Pushed two fix commits (262dab9c addressing range completion validation and SyncProps revalidation; a second commit addressing default today year offset, missing offset-specific outside-month API helper, and same-day range label suffix logic). All Codex threads from both passes were replied to and resolved. CI second run was passing fast lanes; a third Codex pass was pending at session end.

repo: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' on the GitHub issue; the 'snapshot-reviewed' label must be manually reapplied after each push or the snapshot gate blocks.
CI has distinct fast lanes (snapshot, formatting, clippy, unit) and slow lanes (coverage, release verification, Dioxus feature flags) — the slow lanes regularly take 6+ minutes beyond the fast ones.
spec/components/date-time/range-calendar.md is the authoritative source for API shape and default values; mismatches between spec and code are caught by Codex review.
Clippy configuration enforces const fn on new helper methods that are pure — adding a public helper without const fn triggers a -D warnings failure.

code.finding: Default 'today' value was off by one year from the spec contract; requires spec-matched initialization.
Same-day range label logic used an else-if chain, causing the end suffix to be dropped when start == end; start and end suffixes must be built independently.
Api struct was missing the offset-specific is_outside_month helper documented in the spec; must be a const fn to satisfy clippy.
Range completion must validate every date in the contiguous candidate range against the unavailable predicate before committing (first Codex pass, fixed in 262dab9c).
SyncProps must revalidate pending anchor and hover state after constraint/predicate changes and clear stale pending selection when invalid (first Codex pass, fixed in 262dab9c).

workflow: The receiving-code-review skill drives the fix approach: verify each Codex finding against code and spec, write a reproducing test first, then patch the implementation.
Codex review threads must be manually replied to via GitHub API and resolved via GraphQL mutation after each push — they do not auto-resolve.
Polling cadence: 'gh pr checks' + GraphQL review threads query every 120–180s is sufficient to catch state transitions without hammering the API.
cargo llvm-cov is used for coverage checks on the component; running it scoped to the affected module (-- range_calendar) keeps it fast.

friction: Multiple 2–3 minute sleep/poll cycles were required to wait out slow CI lanes (coverage, release verification, Dioxus feature flags), stretching the feedback loop significantly.
Two full Codex trigger/wait/resolve cycles were needed within the session, each requiring manual thread reply and GraphQL resolution.
Clippy enforced const fn on the new offset-specific helper after initial tests passed, requiring a mid-flight patch and re-run.
open_items: Third Codex pass against the second fix commit had not yet completed at session end — outcome unknown.
Final CI lane results for coverage, release verification, and Dioxus feature flags were still pending when the session ended.
